title: If passed, AB 2002 would mandate state contracts involving forest-risk commodities to only go to companies with responsible practices
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 2002 would mandate state contracts involving tropical forest-risk commodities to only go to companies with responsible practices.
The world’s rainforests are in crisis.
Tropical forests cover roughly seven percent of the Earth’s surface but harbor close to half of all species on Earth.
An estimated 18 million acres of forest are lost every year, largely due to the expansion of agribusiness plantations.
Tropical deforestation and related land-use changes are responsible for nearly a quarter of global carbon dioxide emissions.
Tropical forests are essential for stabilizing the global climate.
The rapid loss of tropical forests is a crisis of epic proportions.
